# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function fetchPreview(Request $request)
    {
        $request->validate([
            'url' => 'required|url',
        ]);

        $url = $request->input('url');
        
        $ch = curl_init();
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, $url);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_TIMEOUT, 10);
        
        $content = curl_exec($ch);
        curl_close($ch);
        
        $title = $this->getTitle($content);
        $description = $this->getDescription($content);
        $image = $this->getImage($content);
        
        return response()->json([
            'title' => $title,
            'description' => $description,
            'image' => $image,
            'url' => $url
        ]);
    }

    private function getTitle($content)
    {
        preg_match('/<title>(.*?)<\/title>/si', $content, $matches);
        return $matches[1] ?? 'No title found';
    }

    private function getDescription($content)
    {
        preg_match('/<meta name="description" content="(.*?)"/si', $content, $matches);
        return $matches[1] ?? 'No description found';
    }

    private function getImage($content)
    {
        preg_match('/<meta property="og:image" content="(.*?)"/si', $content, $matches);
        return $matches[1] ?? 'No image found';
    }
}
